meta.purpose: Synchronization of human intent with code semantics

conceptual_model.description: The system implements a state-based synchronization model rather than a simple file copy. The filesystem is treated as a mutable projection of an immutable index state. Data integrity is verified via content hashing (SHA-256), decoupling physical location from logical identity.
conceptual_model.core_abstraction: The 'Index' (index.csv) represents the authoritative state of a directory tree. Operations modify the filesystem to match the Index, or update the Index to match the filesystem.

stage.name: update-index
stage.intent: Capture current filesystem state into the Index.
stage.logic: Scans the directory tree. Computes hashes incrementally (based on mtime/size changes) to optimize performance. Updates the Index to reflect the exact current state of the source directory. Removes records for files and directories that no longer exist on the filesystem. This stage establishes the 'Source of Truth' for subsequent operations.
stage.invariant: After completion, the Index accurately represents the filesystem at the root path. All deleted files/directories are removed from the Index to maintain consistency.

stage.name: reorganize
stage.intent: Align destination structure with source structure without data transfer.
stage.logic: Compares Source Index (desired structure) with Destination Index (current backup state). Identifies files with matching hashes but divergent paths. Plans move (mv) operations to align paths. Identifies empty directories for removal. Does not copy data; only rearranges existing data blocks based on content identity.
stage.constraint: Operates solely on the Destination. Requires Destination Index to be reasonably current.

stage.name: push
stage.intent: Synchronize destination content with source content.
stage.logic: Compares Source Index and Destination Index. Identifies missing files (copy), changed files (update), and removed files (delete). Ensures Destination filesystem matches Source Index state exactly. Handles metadata synchronization (mtime) even if content hash matches.
stage.constraint: Transfers data only when necessary (hash mismatch or missing file).

index_structure.field: Distinguishes between 'file' and 'dir' for structural integrity.
Logical location relative to root. Uses forward slashes for consistency.
Modification time for change detection optimization.
File size for change detection optimization.
SHA-256 content hash. Primary key for data identity.

persistence.mechanism: Atomic writes (write to temp file + rename).
persistence.guarantee: Index corruption prevention during power loss or crashes.
persistence.recovery: Partial writes are discarded; previous valid index remains intact.

consistency.rule: Index is flushed periodically during long operations to minimize data loss on interruption.
Operations are planned fully before execution to allow user confirmation.

safety.constraint: Destructive operations (rm, rmdir) require explicit user confirmation.
Source Index is treated as read-only during reorganize/push.
Symlinks are ignored to prevent recursion loops or security issues.

idempotency.constraint: Running 'push' twice without intermediate changes results in no operations on the second run.
Running 'update-index' twice without filesystem changes results in identical Index state.

data_integrity.constraint: File content is never modified in place; only copied or moved.
Hash mismatches trigger full re-copy, ensuring bit-perfect synchronization.

llm_interpretation_guidelines.modification_context: When analyzing or modifying this code, preserve the separation between Index state and Filesystem state. Do not merge 'reorganize' and 'push' logic; they serve distinct semantic purposes (topology vs. content).
llm_interpretation_guidelines.error_handling: Errors during file operations are logged but do not halt the entire batch (best-effort execution within a run). Index updates are flushed despite errors to maintain state consistency for resumed operations.
llm_interpretation_guidelines.performance_optimization: Hash computation is the bottleneck. Any optimization must preserve the mtime/size check shortcut. Do not remove the incremental hash logic unless explicitly requested, as it is critical for large datasets.
llm_interpretation_guidelines.intent_preservation: The utility is designed for MSYS2/MINGW Windows environments. Path handling (forward slashes, resolve) must remain compatible with this context. Do not introduce OS-specific dependencies that break cross-platform path logic within the intended environment.
